figures.caption: Fig. 1. t-SNE visualization of feature distributions for different physiological modalities and subjects. (a) sEMG features; (b) Pressure features.

Fig. 2. Overview of the proposed PGUDA framework. The left panel presents the multimodal data acquisition and preprocessing pipeline. The middle panel details the two-stage training strategy, including teacher model training and cross-modal knowledge distillation to the student network. The right panel illustrates the inference stage, where only sEMG signals are used for gesture recognition.
Fig. 3. The architecture of the sEMG student network.
Fig. 4. Data acquisition details. (a) The hardware architecture system; (b) Five selected hand gestures.
TABLE I CLASSIFICATION PERFORMANCE (ACCURACY ± STD % AND F1-SCORE ± STD %) IN CROSS-SUBJECT AND CROSS-SESSION EVALUTION WITH DIFFERENT METHODS

Fig. 5. t-SNE visualization of feature distributions with different methods. The top row is the task of Subject 2 → Subject 3, and the bottom row is the task of Session 4 → Session 2. Blue, yellow, green, red, and purple samples denote the hand gestures of hand open, hand grasp, thumbs up, index pointing, and victory sign, respectively.

Fig. 6. Ablation study results of the PGUDA. Subplots (a) and (b) illustrate the classification Accuracy and F1-score across cross-subject and cross-session tasks. The ”Source Only” means the baseline model trained solely labeled source data without any adaptation; The terms PGUDA (w/o Lalign) and PGUDA (w/o Lkd) denote variants where the feature alignment loss and knowledge distillation loss are removed. The full PGUDA model consistently achieves the highest performance, validating the contribution of each component.

Fig. 7. Error rate comparison (Accuracy and F1-score) under different labeled data ratios. The proposed PGUDA (red) consistently achieves lower error rates than the Supervised baseline (blue) across all settings, demonstrating high label efficiency and robust generalization.
Fig. 8. Impact of the trade-off hyperparameter α on model performance. The dual-axis plot displays Accuracy and F1-score for both cross-subject and cross-session protocols.
